description: A designed 'FOR THE RECORD' messaging card from ICE on a deep navy-blue textured (subtle woodgrain) background, matching ICE's branded social-graphic template. Top-left shows the white circular U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal next to white serif text 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement', with a thin dark-red horizontal divider rule beneath. Centered below in very large bold white serif all-caps is the headline 'FOR THE RECORD'. Beneath it, in white serif with the opening phrase bolded, is the definitional statement: 'Targeted enforcement operations are planned arrests of known criminal aliens who threaten national security or public safety.' The card is a rhetorical/definitional 'setting the record straight' graphic designed to preempt or rebut criticism by framing ICE raids as narrowly aimed at 'known criminal aliens.' Authoritative serif typography and official seal convey government legitimacy; not a humorous meme but a deliberate persuasive talking-point card. Target audience is the general public and media skeptical of enforcement operations.
notable_text: FOR THE RECORD — 'Targeted enforcement operations are planned arrests of known criminal aliens who threaten national security or public safety.'
